cvbyte.info: Range 0-127 - (Range 1-255 for Motorola) Default 3
Start voltage Sets the minimum speed of the engine Range 1 - 255 Default 3
This value multiplied by 0.25 is the time from stop to maximum speed. For LokSound 5 DCC: The unit is 0.896 seconds 0 - 255 Default 28
This value multiplied by 0.25 is the time from maximum speed to stop For LokSound 5 DCC: The unit is 0.896 seconds 0 - 255 Default 21
Maximum speed of the engine Range 0 - 255 Default 255
Medium speed of the engine. Use only if 3-point speed table is enabled. For LokSound 5 DCC only.
Version number Internal software version of decoder
ID ESU - Read Default 151
WARNING Writing value 8 in this CV will trigger a reset to factory default values
Motor PWM frequency as a multiple of 1000 Hz. Range 10 - 50 Default 40
Status of functions F1 to F8 in analogue mode (see ESU Man. chapter 12.7) Range 0-255 Default 1
Status of function F0, F9 to F12 in analogue mode (see ESU Man. chapter 12.7) Range 0-63 Default 1
Decoder-Lock Function according to NMRA. For details please see: Range 0 - 255 Default 0
http://www.nmra.org/standards/DCC/WGpublic/0305051/0305051.html Range 0 - 255 Default 0
Long address of engine (see ESU Man. chapter 9.2) Range Bitwise 11000000 to 11100111 Default 11000000 (192)
Long address of engine (see ESU Man. chapter 9.2) Range Bitwise 00000000 to 11111111 (0 - 255) Default 0

Additional address for consist operation. Value 0 or 128 means: consist address is disabled. Active consist address Range for Forward 1 - 127. Active consist address Range for Reverse 129 - 255. Default 0
Status of functions F1 to F8 in Consist mode Meaning of the bits as in CV 13 Range 0-255 Default 0
Status of functions FL, F9 to F12 in Consist mode Meaning of the bits as in CV 14 Range 0-63 Default 0
Values from 0 to 127 are added to CV 3. If the values are to be subtracted, additionally set bit 7 (value 128). The unit is 0.896 seconds. Range 0 - 127 Default 0
Values from 0 to 127 are added to CV 3. If the values are to be subtracted, additionally set bit 7 (value 128). The unit is 0.896 seconds. Range 0 - 127 Default 0

dipvalue.name: ABC braking, voltage higher on the right hand side
ABC braking, voltage higher on the left hand side
Brake on DC, if polarity against driving direction
Brake on DC, if polarity like driving direction
Selectrix brake diode, brakes if polarity is against driving direction
Selectrix brake diode, rakes if polarity is like driving direction
Loco brakes with constant brake distance if Speed=0
RailCom Plus automatic loco recognition active

cvbyte.info: Index Register H Selection page for CV257-512. For LokSound 5 usually set to 16 Default=16
Index Register L Selection page for CV257-512. For LokSound 5 Range 0 - 16 Default=0

adip.title: Which decoder protocols are active. Please see Loksound 5 manual chapter 9.5.

dipvalue.name: M4 protocol active (Not for LokSound 5 DCC)
Motorola protocol active (Not for LokSound 5 DCC)
Selectric protocol active (Not for LokSound 5 DCC)

cvbyte.info: Extended Configuration #1 Range 0 - 255 Default 19
Internal Speedstep, until K Slow is active Range 0-255 Default=10
K -Portion of the PI-Controller valid for lower speed steps Range 0-255 Default=10
Defines the Back EMF voltage, which the motor should generate at maximum speed Range 0-255 Default=130
K–component of the internal PI-controller. Defines the effect of load control. The higher the value, the stronger the effect of Back EMF control. Range 0 - 255 Default=50

«I»–component of the internal PI-controller. Defines the momentum (inertia) of the motor. The higher the momentum of the motor (large flywheel or bigger motor), the lower this value has to be set. Range 0 - 255 Default=100
Defines the »Strengh« of the BEMF at minimum speed step Range 1 - 255 Default=255
Defines the steam chuff synchronization. See chapter 13.3. Range 1-255 Default=30
Defines the steam chuff synchronization. See chapter 13.3. Range 1-255 Default=30
Master volume for all sounds. Range 0-192 Default=128
If the actual loco speed step is smaller than or equals the value indicated here, the brake sound is triggered. Compare chapter 13.4. Range 0 - 255 Default=60
If the actual loco speed step is smaller than the one indicated here (up to 255), the brake sound will be switched off again. Compare chapter 13.4. Range 0 - 255 Default=7
Forward Trim Divided by 128 is the factor used to multiply the motor voltage when driving forward. The value 0 deactivates the trim. Range 0 - 255 Default=128
Reverse Trim Divided by 128 is the factor used to multiply the motor voltage when driving backwards. Value 0 deactivates the trim. Range 0 - 255 Default=128
Saves the CV number that should be actually programmed in hundreds. See manual section 8.2.8/9 Range 0 - 9
Saves the CV number that should be actually programmed in units and tens. See manual section 8.2.8/9 Range 0 - 99
Saves the value of the CV that should be actually programmed in hundreds. See manual section 8.2.9 Range 0 - 9
Saves the CV number that should be actually programmed in units and tens. See manual section 8.2.8/9 Range 0 - 99
Shunting Mode Trim Divided by 128, this gives the factor by which the motor voltage is multiplied when the shunting gear is active. See section 10.1.2. Range 0 - 128 Default=64
Brake Mode Exit Delay Time as a multiple of 16 milliseconds that must pass before a detected braking distance is left again. See section 10.4.6. Range 0 - 255 Default=12
Load adjustment »Optional Load” Divided by 128, this gives the factor that changes CV3, CV4 and the sound when »Optional Load« is active. See section 10.7. Range 0 - 255 Default=0
Load adjustment »Primary Load” Divided by 128, this gives the factor that changes CV3, CV4 and the sound when »Primry Load« is active. See section 10.7. Range 0 - 255 Default=255
Here you are able to save what ever you want. Range 0 - 255 Default=0
Here you are able to save what ever you want. Range 0 - 255 Default=0
Gearbox backlash Time as a multiple of 16 mS, for which the motor runs at minimum speed after reversing the direction to prevent gear box jerking. Range 0 - 255 Default=0
Flashing frequency for Strobe lighting effects.Multiple of 0.065536 seconds. See section 12.5.4. Range 0 - 255 Default=20
The time that the decoder bridges via the PowerPack after an interruption of voltage. Unit: A multiple of 0.032768 sec. See section 6.12.2. Range 0 - 255 Default=32
Frequency of BEMF measurement in 0.1 milliseconds at speed step 1 Range 50 - 200 Default=50
Frequency of BEMF measurement in 0.1 milliseconds at speed step 255 Range 50 - 200 Default=150

cvbyte.desc: Slow speed BEMF Measurement gap length
cvbyte.info: VMin Length of the BEMF measuring gap in 0.1 milliseconds at speed step 1 Range 10 - 20 Default=15

cvbyte.desc: Full speed BEMF Measurement gap length
cvbyte.info: Vmax Length of the BEMF measuring gap in 0.1 milliseconds at speed step 255 Range 10 - 20 Default=15
Speed which is valid in the slow driving section during ABC braking. Range 0 - 255 Default=100
Additional important settings for decoders - Default=21

dipvalue.name: Bi-directional bit: Keep driving direction when changing direction.
Disable=Motor Overload Protection Motor is not switched off when blocked. Enable=Motor is switched off for a few seconds when blocked to avoid burnout

cvbyte.info: See manual section 10.8. Range 0 - 255 Default=90
See manual section 10.8. Range 0 - 255 Default=130
See manual section 10.8. Range 0 - 255 Default=90
See manual section 10.8. Range 0 - 255 Default=130
Offset voltage for functions in Analog mode. Chapter 10.8. Range 0 - 255 Default=15
Offset voltage for motor functions in Analog mode. Chapter 10.8. Range 0 - 255 Default=5
Grade Crossing holding time. See manual chapter 12.5.3. Range 0 - 255 Default=80
Volume when sound fader is active. See chapter 13.5. Range 0 - 255 Default=128
ABC-Mode Sensitivity Threshold, from which asymmentry on ABC shall be recognized. Range 4 - 32 Default=10
Smoke Unit Trim Fan Divided by 128, this gives the factor by which the fan speed of synchronized smoke units can be adjusted. Range 0 - 255 Default=128
Smoke Unit Trim Temperature Divided by 128, this gives the factor by which the temperature of synchronized smoke units can be adjusted. Range 0 - 255 Default=128
Smoke TimeOut Time until automatic shutdown of the smoke unit. Range 0 - 255 Default=255
Smoke Chuff Min Minimum duration of a steam chuff of an external smoke unit in 0.041 seconds resolution. Range 0 - 255 Default=10
Smoke Chuff max Maximum duration of a steam chuff of an external smoke unit in 0.041 seconds resolution. Range 0 - 255 Default=125
Smoke Chuff Length Divided by 128, this gives the factor by which the duration of the steam chuffs can be adjusted relative to the trigger pulses. Range 0 - 255 Default=100
Smoke Pre Heat Temperature Preheating temperature in degrees Celsius for secondary smoke generators (cylinder smoke unit) Range 0 - 255 Default=150
ABC Shuttle Train Holdtime Time in seconds, which has to be passed for ABC shuttle train operation, before the direction of travel is changed. See section 10.4.4.3. Range 0 - 255 Default=255
HLU Speed limit 1. Internal speedstep. Range 0 - 255 Default=42
HLU Speed limit 2 (U). Internal speedstep. Range 0 - 255 Default=85
HLU Speed limit 3. Internal speedstep. Range 0 - 255 Default=127
HLU Speed limit 4 (L). Internal speedstep. Range 0 - 255 Default=170
HLU Speed limit 5. Internal speedstep. Range 0 - 255 Default=212
16 CVs for selecting sounds that can be assigned within sound projects. Please note the documentation for the sound project. Range 0 - 255 Default=0
Deceleration Value of which 33% of CV 4 will be deducted if the Brake Function 1 is active. See section 10.6. Range 0 - 255 Default=80
Deceleration Value of which 33% of CV 4 will be deducted if the Brake Function 2 is active. See section 10.6. Range 0 - 255 Default=40
Deceleration Value of which 33% of CV 4 will be deducted if the Brake Function 3 is active. See section 10.6. Range 0 - 255 default=40
max. Speed Highest speed step that can be reached when Brake function 1 is active. Range 0 - 126 Default=0
max. Speed Highest speed step that can be reached when Brake function 1 is active. Range 0 - 126 Default=126
max. Speed Highest speed step that can be reached when Brake function 1 is active. Range 0 - 126 Default=126

Speed of the loco while decoupling; the higher the value, the faster the loco. Value 0 switches the automatic coupler off. Automatic decoupling is only active if the function output is adjusted to pulse or coupler. Range 0 - 255 default=0
This value multiplied with 0.016 defines the time the loco needs for moving away from the train (automatic decoupling). Range 0 – 255 default=0
This value multiplied with 0.016 defines the time the loco needs for pushing against the train (automatic decoupling). Range 0 – 255 default=0
Minimum distance of two steam chuffs, independent from sensor data. See chapter 13.3. Range 0 – 255 Default=0

Defines the distance between two consecutive steam chuffs for the secondary steam chuff generator. The value indicates the promilles the steam chuff distances of the secondary steam chuff generator ought to be shorter then those of the primary steam chuff generator. It is needed for steam locos with two independent engines, such as Big Boy or Mallet. Range 0 – 255 Default=0
Determines the constant brake mode. If CV 253 = 0: Decoder stops linearly, if CV 253 > 0: Decoder stops constantly linear. Only active, if CV254 > 0 Range 0 – 255 Default=0
A value > 0 determines the way of brake distance it adheres to, independent from speed. Range 0 – 255 Default=0
Constant braking distances during reverse driving. Only active, if value > 0, otherwise the value of CV 254 is used. Useful for reversible trains. Range 0 – 255 Default=0
